title: Recurrent otitis media history
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric, audiology, and ENT adaptation
source.population: Children under 12 years with suspected or confirmed otitis media with effusion and their parents or carers.
source.setting: Primary, paediatric, audiology, and ENT recognition, assessment, reassessment, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for paediatric OME-associated hearing, communication, balance, mouth-breathing and adenoid context; otoscopy, hearing testing, tympanometry, reassessment, and post-grommet review. It does not support acute otitis media, adults, automatic treatment, or a procedure decision.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

assessment.text: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.
assessment.rationale: The exact paediatric AOM section supports episode timing, current symptom impact, associated features, relevant negatives, rapid worsening or systemic context, and clinician reassessment without diagnosing the current episode.

source.title: Otitis media (acute): antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute otitis media; partial for a broad pediatric ear-pain workflow and not directly validated for UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

assessment.text: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.
assessment.rationale: The exact paediatric OME section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment and review of hearing or tympanometry results when recurrent AOM history raises hearing or effusion concern; it does not diagnose OME or recommend tubes. The exact paediatric OME section supports reviewing AOM history, hearing, language, balance, otoscopy, hearing testing, and tympanometry; no broad legacy mapping was added because the workflow lacks age and OME qualifiers.

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
